armyStats.acr: Average CR of units composing the army
armyStats.om: ACR + commander bonus + troop type bonus

abilities: Inspiring Word (+1 morale to adjacent armies once per battle)
Rally the Troops (end rout once per battle)
Legendary Commander (+2 OM and DV to all armies)

specialAbilities.name: Breath Weapon
specialAbilities.description: Once per battle, +3 OM for one round

specialAbilities.name: Burn
specialAbilities.description: Fire damage persists (1 hp per round)

specialAbilities.name: Construct
specialAbilities.description: Immune to morale, no OM penalties from morale

specialAbilities.name: Disease
specialAbilities.description: Can inflict sickness on opposing army

specialAbilities.name: Fear
specialAbilities.effect: Target must make morale check or -2 OM

specialAbilities.name: Grab
specialAbilities.description: Can grapple and restrain enemy armies

specialAbilities.name: Mindless
specialAbilities.description: Immune to morale effects and compulsion

triggers: Flanked by two or more armies
moraleChecks.failureEffect: Army takes -2 penalty on OM and DV until end of battle
moraleChecks.routEffect: If morale drops below -4 or army fails two consecutive checks, army routs and flees
moraleChecks.routRecovery: One hour rest and successful Diplomacy or Intimidate check (DC 20) by commander

victoryPoints.description: Armies earn VP by achieving objectives. First to set limit wins.
victoryPoints.vpPerRound: 1 VP for winning the round (OM > enemy OM)

armyRepair.description: Armies recover HP with rest and resources
armyRepair.perDay: ACR x 2 HP recovered per day
armyRepair.withHealer: ACR x 3 HP recovered per day
armyRepair.fullRecovery: ACR days of rest required for full HP

armyConsumption.description: Food and supplies consumed per week
armyConsumption.baseFormula: Army Size x consumption rating = units per week
armyConsumption.shortage: Army takes -1 morale and loses 1d4 HP per week without supplies
armyConsumption.storage: Army can carry 2 weeks of supplies (requires supply wagons for more)

armySpeed.description: Distance traveled per day of march
armySpeed.ForcedMarch: Double distance but -1 morale and ACR x 1 HP loss per day

fameInfamyScale.description: Combined fame and infamy score from 0-55 points per region
fameInfamyScale.famePoints: Earned by heroic deeds, good news, public victories
fameInfamyScale.infamyPoints: Earned by villainous acts, criminal activity, public defeats

fameTiers.benefits: +2 Diplomacy in home settlement, can purchase items 10% below cost
+2 Diplomacy in region, free lodging in settlements, establish contacts
+3 Diplomacy in region, audiences with minor nobles
+4 Diplomacy regionally, noble audiences, request favors from officials
+4 Diplomacy widely, military units may offer allegiance, legends written
+5 Diplomacy everywhere, kingdoms negotiate with you
+6 Diplomacy everywhere, can demand armies, political power

infamyTiers.effects: +1 on Intimidate, -1 on Diplomacy with law-abiding
+2 Intimidate, -2 Diplomacy with law-abiding, guards watchful
+4 Intimidate, -4 Diplomacy, bounty hunters, refused service
+6 Intimidate, -6 Diplomacy, armies sent, assassins hired
Kingdoms unite against you, crusades declared, no mercy

spendFame.description: Purchase rare item at normal price (normally unavailable)
Train in new skill with +2 bonus

benefits: Access to religious texts and relics

drawbacks: Subject to military chain of command

honorCode.description: Honor represents a character's adherence to a personal or cultural code of conduct.
honorCode.gainPerLevel: +1 per level if maintaining honor code

bonuses: +1 morale on attacks vs dishonorable foes
Can spend 1 honor to reroll failed save vs compulsion
Allies gain +1 morale when you lead
Can spend 1 honor to cure charm effect
Gain info from criminal networks easier
Can spend 1 honor to make one attack as full attack
violations: Use poison/magic on equal opponent: -4 honor

honorBenefits.benefit: +1 morale bonus on saves vs fear
honorBenefits.special: Morale bonus increases to +2 at 25 honor

honorBenefits.benefit: +1 morale bonus on attack rolls when defending allies
Once per day, reroll a failed save (must take second result)
honorBenefits.special: Use twice per day at 50 honor

honorBenefits.benefit: +2 morale bonus on saves vs compulsion and charm
Allies within 30 ft gain +1 morale on attack rolls and saves vs fear
Gain +1 effective level for leadership purposes
Once per week, auto-success on single attack roll
honorBenefits.special: Use twice per week at 85 honor

honorBenefits.benefit: +1 to all saves, gains legendary status

dishonerThresholds.effect: -2 to all saves and attacks, -1 to AC
Loses all honor-based abilities, allies suffer -1 morale
Automatically fail against honorable opponents in single combat

atonementMechanics.description: Restore lost honor through great deeds
requirements: Perform the action that caused dishonor but correctly
Quest set by honor code arbiter

maintainTrust.action: Deliver message, small favor, or gift worth 50+ gp
maintainTrust.failure: Trust decreases by 1 level per month of inactivity

consequences: Spreads rumors about betrayer (-2 to reputation checks)
Access denied to all contacts in network
Price on head among underworld (500-5000 gp)
betrayalMechanics.redemption: Requires major favor, apology, 2-4 weeks time, and successful DC 20 Diplomacy check

contactDeparture.death: Ally must avenge or accept loss
contactDeparture.imprisonment: Requires rescue or bail to maintain contact
contactDeparture.promotion: Contact rises in position (may become patron instead)
contactDeparture.retirement: Contact leaves profession (becomes enemy or friend)

caseComplexity.examples: Missing item, minor theft, simple disappearance
Large conspiracy, serial crimes, organized crime
Powerful organization, god-touched event, world-shaking plot

clueTypes.name: Physical Evidence
clueTypes.description: Footprints, blood, damage, items left behind

clueTypes.name: Witness Testimony
clueTypes.description: What people saw, heard, or know

clueTypes.name: Documentary Evidence
clueTypes.investigation: Read Language or Decipher Script DC 15-25

researchRules.assistBonus: +2 per assistant with relevant Knowledge skill
researchRules.timeToAccess: 1-24 hours depending on library and popularity

redHerrings.description: False clues that lead away from truth
redHerrings.implant: Intentionally placed by criminal or by chance
redHerrings.consequence: Following leads to dead ends and wasted time
redHerrings.resolution: Recognize inconsistency with other clues or find contrary evidence

deductionSystem.clueConnection: Connect 2+ clues for insight (Logic check DC 12-20)
deductionSystem.falseConclu­sion: Wrong deduction leads to false arrest or wild goose chase
deductionSystem.correctConclusion: Deduction narrows down suspects or motive

interrogation.falseConfession: Poor technique leads to innocent confession

chainOfCustody.description: Maintain evidence integrity for legal proceedings

madnessEffects.name: Phobia
madnessEffects.effect: Shaken when near trigger, panicked on close contact

madnessEffects.name: Paranoia
madnessEffects.effect: -4 on Diplomacy/Sense Motive, cannot benefit from Aid Another

madnessEffects.name: Hallucinations
madnessEffects.effect: 50% chance per round of acting on hallucination

madnessEffects.name: Amnesia
madnessEffects.effect: Lose all skill ranks, feats, class abilities (retain BAB, saves, HP)

madnessEffects.name: Mania/Depression
madnessEffects.effect: Alternating rounds of reckless bravery (+2 attack, -2 AC) and despair (-2 all)

madnessEffects.name: Dissociative Disorder
madnessEffects.effect: Alternate personality takes over 25% of the time (GM controls)

madnessEffects.name: Psychosis
madnessEffects.effect: Cannot distinguish friend from foe, 50% chance to attack nearest

madnessEffects.name: Compulsion
madnessEffects.effect: Must perform action (counting, touching, speaking) or take -2 all

madnessEffects.name: Depression
madnessEffects.effect: -4 to Charisma-based checks, -2 HP per day (voluntary), suicidal thoughts

madnessEffects.name: Obsession
madnessEffects.effect: Fixates on single topic, -4 concentration checks on other tasks

madnessEffects.name: Delusion
madnessEffects.effect: Believes false information, acts based on delusion regardless of evidence

sanityMilestones.effect: Character feels uneasy, -1 to concentration checks
Character feels paranoid, -2 to Sense Motive, gains Phobia or mild hallucination
Character feels desperate, -4 to social checks, prone to recklessness
Permanent insanity, must make daily Will save vs new madness effect

recoveryMethods.description: 1 sanity point per week of rest in safe conditions
Restoration restores 2d6, Greater Restoration restores all, Heal restores 3d6
Heal check DC 20 once per week, success restores 1d4 sanity
Completing quest that caused trauma restores 1d6 sanity

indefiniteMadness.triggers: Multiple permanent insanities or reaching 0 sanity twice
indefiniteMadness.effects: Lose 1 ability point per day, cumulative -2 to all checks per permanent madness
indefiniteMadness.recovery: Wish, Miracle, or completing major character arc

groupSanity.massPanic: One character's failed save triggers group panic save DC 15 (fail = flee)
groupSanity.sharedDelusion: Group of characters can all accept same delusion, reinforcing each other
groupSanity.morale: Party sanity loss applies -1 morale to combat actions if loss is recent

magicItemCreation.baseTime: 1 day per 1,000 gp of base cost (minimum 1 day)
magicItemCreation.failurePenalty: Lose materials (half cost) on natural 1, cursed item created on critical failure

magicItemTypes.baseCost: spell level x CL x 25 gp
spell level x CL x 12.5 gp
spell level x CL x 375 gp (50 charges)

mundaneCrafting.time: 1 day per 10 gp of finished item cost

masterworkCrafting.additionalCheck: Separate Craft check DC 20 after base item complete
masterworkCrafting.bonus: +1 to relevant attack or skill check using item

specialMaterials.properties: Damage reduction 1/- for weapons, hardness 20, weight -10%
Overcomes fey and some outsider DR, -1 to magic properties
Weight -50%, +1 dodge AC if armor, strong vs magic
Weight -50%, +1 dodge AC, +1 to magic properties
Can be treated like armor material, lighter
Magical affinity, +1 to spell DCs of caster using items
Necromantic affinity, light weight, some DR bypasses

failedCrafting.effect: Item functions at -1 to bonuses, 10% chance of failure per use
Item has negative effect (see cursed items table), -2 to saves
Item functions opposite of intended, deals damage to user
Item disintegrates after 1d6 uses, rolls naturally
Item possessed by harmful entity, -4 to attacks/skill checks
Deals 2d6 damage to crafter, destroys all materials

cooperativeCrafting.description: Multiple crafters work together on single item
cooperativeCrafting.bonus: +1 per assistant with relevant Craft skill
cooperativeCrafting.minimumDC: Base DC -2 if two crafters of equal skill

haggleRules.failurePenalty: Merchant refuses to negotiate further for 24 hours

auctionMechanics.bidding: Starting bid 50% of estimate, increments of 5-10%
auctionMechanics.rivalBidders: 1-3 other bidders (DC 15 opposed Appraise to estimate their budget)
auctionMechanics.communalSale: All parties interested in item must bid

blackMarket.availability: Illegal items available (weapons, poisons, stolen goods)
blackMarket.detection: Perception vs Stealth check DC 15 to identify undercover guards
blackMarket.penalty: Caught = arrest or violence depending on item

cheatingDetection.detectionCheck: Perception or Sense Motive (DC = cheater's Sleight of Hand)
cheatingDetection.penalty: Expelled from game, reputation loss, possible violence (-5 to all Charisma checks in settlement)

gamblingResults.result: Bad loss — lose your full bet
Narrow win — win equal to your bet
Jackpot — win 3x your bet (chance of trouble)

disasters.name: Earthquake
disasters.aftereffects: Rubble (difficult terrain), fires, aftershocks 50% next 1d4 hours

disasters.name: Flood
disasters.aftereffects: Mud (difficult terrain), disease risk (Fort DC 14), economic disruption

disasters.name: Wildfire
disasters.aftereffects: Smoke inhalation (Fort DC 15 or nauseated), 1d4 days to clear, starvation risk

disasters.name: Tornado
disasters.aftereffects: Debris field, destroyed structures, 10d10 damage to buildings

disasters.name: Tsunami
disasters.aftereffects: Devastation zone, disease, starvation (massive economic impact -50% trade)

disasters.name: Blizzard
disasters.aftereffects: Snow drifts (difficult terrain), hypothermia risk, food scarcity, isolation

disasters.name: Volcanic Eruption
disasters.aftereffects: Ash fall (visibility 5 ft, suffocation risk), pyroclastic flow (instant death), years of darkness

disasters.name: Plague Outbreak
disasters.aftereffects: Population loss (roll 1d6x10% dead), quarantine (trade -75%), economic collapse

disasters.name: Meteor Strike
disasters.aftereffects: Crater, fires everywhere, dust storm (visibility 5 ft for 1d4 days), earthquakes

disasters.name: Famine
disasters.aftereffects: Starvation (1d6% population dies), poverty, crime increase, economic depression

disasters.name: Drought
disasters.aftereffects: Water scarcity, disease increase, wildfires (cascading disaster)

disasters.name: Hurricane
disasters.aftereffects: Storm surge, flooding, destroyed buildings, disease

drugs.name: Flayleaf
drugs.effect: +2 alchemical bonus on saves vs mind-affecting, -2 WIS for 1 hour

drugs.name: Pesh
drugs.effect: +1d8 temp HP, -2 DEX and WIS for 1 hour

drugs.name: Shiver
drugs.effect: +2 alchemical bonus to CHA-based checks, euphoria for 1d4 hours

drugs.name: Zerk
drugs.effect: +1 alchemical bonus to initiative, +1d4 temp HP for 1 hour

drugs.name: Opium
drugs.effect: +1d8 temp HP, -2 STR and DEX, fatigue immunity for 1 hour

drugs.name: Bloodhaze
drugs.effect: +2 to damage rolls, vision turns red, -2 AC for 2 hours

drugs.name: Lotus
drugs.effect: Euphoria, +1 to social skills, -2 to Perception for 4 hours

drugs.name: Dreamweaver
drugs.effect: Can see invisible creatures, -2 to attack rolls for 3 hours

drugs.name: Lich Dust
drugs.effect: +2 to Knowledge checks, -2 HP per round for 1 hour (up to 10), immunity to fear

drugs.name: Ghastbloom
drugs.effect: Appear dead for 1d4 hours, can see undead, -2 CHA

drugs.name: Duskfire
drugs.effect: Sunlight sensitivity removed for 1 hour, gain darkvision 60 ft
drugs.overdose: Burn from inside (5d6 fire damage), 2d4 CON damage

drugs.name: Essence of Void
drugs.effect: Gain 1d4 negative levels for 2 hours, immune to positive energy
drugs.overdose: Become undead or die, Fortitude save DC 30

npcBoons.boon: Free patrol of party's property once per week
Free healing (up to 3rd level spells) once per week
Introduction to other nobles or officials
Free Knowledge check assistance (+4 bonus) once per week
One piece of local underworld intelligence per week
Free room and board for party
20% discount on alchemical items and potions
Research magical item properties (1/month) or scroll preparation
Access to entertainment venues and gather rumors in any settlement
Once per week bestow temporary blessing (+1 to saves for 1 day)
Find safe haven in wilderness, free herbalism knowledge
Emergency aid if party calls (appears in 1d6 days)

alignmentShift.description: Characters may change alignment based on major actions
alignmentShift.recovery: +1 per week of atonement or good actions

atonementSpell.effect: Shift alignment up to 2 steps toward desired alignment

bloodlines.name: Celestial
bloodlines.description: Descended from angels or heavenly beings
traits: +2 to social interactions with good-aligned beings
abilities: At level 5: Smite evil 1/day (+damage equal to CHA mod)
drawbacks: Evil beings get +2 save vs your spells

bloodlines.name: Draconic
bloodlines.description: Descended from dragons or draconic beings
abilities: Breathe fire (as young dragon, 1d6 per level) 1/day at level 3
Resistance to elemental damage (based on color)

bloodlines.name: Infernal
bloodlines.description: Descended from devils or demonic beings
abilities: Summon minor infernal ally 1/day at level 5

bloodlines.name: Fey
bloodlines.description: Descended from fey folk or nature spirits
abilities: Spell resistance equal to 10 + character level

bloodlines.name: Undead
bloodlines.description: Descended from undead or cursed bloodline
abilities: Can survive on negative energy instead of food

bloodlines.name: Aberrant
bloodlines.description: Descended from aberrations or alien beings

characterRetirement.description: Character exits active play to begin new phase of life
benefits: Can be called on for aid in emergencies
Legacy bonuses for new characters (see below)

legacyBonuses.description: New character can inherit benefits from retired ancestor/mentor
legacyBonuses.mentorFeats: Learn one feat from mentor's list at character creation
legacyBonuses.mentorTrain: Mentor provides free training (2 weeks) in one skill (+3 bonus)
legacyBonuses.mentorAlliance: Mentor's allies become allies of new character (+2 Diplomacy)

routeEstablishment.description: Party establishes regular trade route between settlements
routeEstablishment.startupCost: 500-2000 gp depending on distance and infrastructure

banditryRisk.roadHazard: 1-5% chance caravan encounters bandits per trip
banditryRisk.lossOnAttack: 25-75% of cargo lost if bandits win
banditryRisk.protection: Hire guards (+10% cost) reduces loss to 10%
banditryRisk.insurance: Purchase caravan insurance at 5% of cargo value

caravanRules.speed: 20 miles per day (slower than normal travel)

spyMechanics.recruitment: Diplomacy/Intimidate DC 15-25 to turn NPC spy
spyMechanics.reliability: Contact trust level determines likelihood of success

sabotageRules.discovery: If caught, immediate combat or escape required

counterIntelligence.detection: Opposed Sense Motive vs Bluff for lies
counterIntelligence.exposure: Spy is captured or killed, network disrupted

growth.description: Children grow into adulthood during campaign
growth.physiqueAdvance: Gain +1 ability point every 2 years in category
growth.skillAdvance: Gain 1 skill rank per year of aging
growth.levelRequirement: Cannot gain class levels above certain age (adult = normal levels)
